# Purple Team Lab — Neerav Patel

A working lab documenting offensive (red team), defensive (blue team), digital forensics, cyber crime OSINT, and independent security research. Built and maintained as an active practice log — not a one-time portfolio dump.

**Portfolio site:** https://founder-neeravpatel.github.io
**Contact:** xcybersquad@gmail.com · [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/neerav-patel-b73191265)

---

## Lab Structure

| Folder | What's inside |
|---|---|
| [`lab-setup/`](./lab-setup) | How this lab is physically built — VM setup, network isolation, tooling install |
| [`red-team/`](./red-team) | Offensive methodology, recon → exploitation → post-exploitation tooling notes |
| [`blue-team/`](./blue-team) | Detection engineering, SIEM (Splunk) use cases, incident response process |
| [`osint/`](./osint) | OSINT & social media investigation methodology for cyber crime cases |
| [`forensics/`](./forensics) | Digital forensics workflow, chain of custody, tooling |
| [`research/`](./research) | CVE research notes, malware analysis writeups |
| [`writeups/`](./writeups) | Case studies from lab exercises (template-based, anonymized) |

## Why this lab exists

Most security portfolios show either offense or defense. This one is built around the purple team idea — that testing and detection should inform each other. Every red team technique documented here is paired with how a blue team would catch it; every forensics case is approached the way an investigation would actually run, from evidence handling to reporting.

## Certifications backing this lab

- CEH — Certified Ethical Hacker
- Splunk Certification (SIEM / Log Analysis)
- Defence Analyst & Operations Certification
- Counter-Terrorism Certification

## Disclaimer

All work in this lab is conducted in isolated/authorized environments (personal VMs, authorized test ranges, CTF platforms, or with explicit client authorization). No content here targets real, non-consenting systems or individuals. This repository does not contain exploit code, malware samples, or operational attack tooling — it documents methodology, tool categories, and findings only.
